calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Kenneth Moye.
David Tate for and with Victor Moye (Trustee).
Alice Moye in Propria Persona.

Mr. Tate stated that there is a settlement with a provision that his Client buyout the house, that his Client got funding, that a check has been given to Kenneth and that they are having difficulty with the other two Beneficiaries. Mr. Tate also stated that they asked all three of the Beneficiaries to sign a quit claim deed and that all of the Parties were in settlement. Ms. Calhoun raised the issue of Victor not arranging this through escrow, stated that he did not distribute shares to the Beneficiaries, that they found out Victor holds 100% title and that there is the issue of the jewelry for which there is no word about. Ms. Calhoun also stated that the issue of the monument/grave site marking is still up in the air and requested a continuance. Mr. Tate stated that the issues in the Trust petition are now moot. Ms. Calhoun noted that there is a provision to enforce the settlement. The Petition for Instructions, etc. is off calendar. The Court encouraged the Parties to do what ever it takes get the settlement completed and advised Ms. Moye to hire a Lawyer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Continued To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Continued To Sep-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Continued To Jul-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Continued To Jul-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Parnall for and with Kenneth Moye (Petitioner/Beneficiary)
David Tate for and with Victor Moye (Trustee)
Ms. Ng, non-party, present in court (Beneficiary)

Mr. Parnell stated that they are open to settling, they've already had 2 mediations, a final settlement to resolve everything remains elusive, has filed a short reply, several issues remain on the table, and would like to have a private fiduciary take over as Trustee. Mr. Tate stated that he viewed this case as being settled, there is a settlement draft everybody agreed to, recommends a mandatory settlement conference with the Court, the law does not support bringing a professional for successor trustee, and his client did nothing wrong. The Court expressed concerns that private issues should not be combined with Trustee issues, that he is not inclined to suspend the Trustee today, and inquired if all parties are open to settlement conference. Mr. Tate stated that he wants a mandatory settlement conference. Mr. Parnell stated that they have already done settlement conferences twice, key issues are not resolved, and would be open to settlement conference with Judge Busch's assistance. The Court stated that an adjudicator should not hear non-admissible/emotional pleas which may come out during settlement conference. The Court instructed parties to get in touch with Settlement Commissioner, Pang Ly, to arrange for a settlement conference date, and submit settlement brief/report. All parties and counsel are to be present for the settlement conference, and to hold all discovery until mandatory settlement conference is done. The Petition for Instructions, etc. is continued to July 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions, Including Orders: (1)Declaring Victor Moyes Creditors Claim To Be Invalid; (2)Surcharging Trustee Victor Moye; (3)Instructing Victor Moye To Vacate Trust Real Property; (4)Suspending Victor Moye'S Trustee Powers And Appointing Temporary Professional Fiduciary, Or Appointing A Trustee Ad Litem; (5)Instructing The Trustee, (6)Awarding Petitioner His Reasonable Attorney Fees; (7)Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Apr-24-2018 Continued To May-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Property
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: David Tate for and with Victor Moye (Beneficiary). David Parnall for Kenneth Moye (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it now has the bank statements which leaves the merits of the 850 petition. Mr. Tate stated that it was his understanding that there was no opposition to the 850 petition. The Court stated that it had no problem with the assets set forth in Exhibit A but that it didn't see evidence to support the other relief that was being sought. Mr. Tate stated that they were fine with the 850 petition with the scheduled assets less the $150,000.00 which could be dealt with separately. The Court inquired whether Mr. Tate was stating that rest of the petition would remain alive. Mr. Tate confirmed that the rest of the petition would not remain alive. The Court then granted Victor Moye's Petition For Order Determining Ownership Of Property with the exhibits set forth in Exhibit A - otherwise denied (subject to review of the order). The Court noted that it would make sure that the assets set forth in paragraph 1 & 2 line up with what is reflected in Exhibit A and that the petition is granted to that extent. Mr. Parnall stated that there were matters/issues that he wished to get on calendar and that he would contact the Examiner.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Tate for Victor Moye.
David Parnall for Ken Moye.
Don Sweet for Alice Ng (Interested Party).

The Court noted that it does not have a problem with a group of assets identified in exhibit-A, but it has concerns about property identified in exhibit-A that the parties want the Court to determine that they are not in the Trust, and a group of assets that are not listed in exhibit-A that the parties want the Court to determine that they are in the Trust. The Court also stated that if the parties want the Court's involvement, there has to be a basis. Mr. Tate stated that he did not realize this was an issue, that he can provide a declaration from the parties (his Client, Mr. Parnall's Client, Family members), or they can work on getting the parties to agree on this. Mr. Tate also stated that his Client is out of town and that he was unable to obtain the bank statements. The Petition for Order Determining Ownership of Property is continued to December 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Continued To Oct-16-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Petition For Orders A) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Named Successor; And B) Compelling Trustee Vitor Moye To : 1) Provide Trust Accounting; 2) Re
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Continued To Oct-16-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Tate for and with Victor Moye; David Parnall for and with Kenneth Moye.

The Court asked if the matter was headed to an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Tate responded that an evidentiary hearing was needed. Mr. Parnall stated his objection to the Status Report on the grounds it contained information protected from disclosure under the mediation privilege, and in violation of the settlement agreement of the parties. He contended that there is a dispute among the parties as to the propriety of actions taken by Victor Moye as trustee, and that a neutral trustee should step in to protect the interests of the other trust beneficiaries. According to Mr. Parnall, Scott Hodges is willing to step in to act as a neutral trustee. Mr. Tate responded that the only real issue to be determined is Victor Moye's right to reimbursement for funds advanced on behalf of his father. The Court stated he could not make a determination on the petition in light of the demand for an evidentiary hearing, and inquired as to the number of witnesses expected to testify. Mr. Tate stated that he wanted to conduct three depositions, and expected to call 4-5 witnesses at the evidentiary hearing. Mr. Parnall said he wopuld also have 4-5 witnesses. The Court concluded that it was unlikely the matter could be heard in its entirety in two days, and ordered the two petitions transferred to Department 206 for a three day bench trial commencing October 16, 2017. The Court said that a Mandatory Settlement Conference date would be set by the Court, with notice to the parties when the date is determined. The Court set a deadline of September 22, 2017 for completion of mediation through ADR. Mr. Parnall suggested that matters could be short cut if the Court would exercise its discretionary powers to suspend the trustee. The Court responded that as Victor Moye had demanded an evidentiary hearing, the Court needed to permit the limited discovery to go forward and establish the merits of each side's position. The Court further set a Status Hearing re mediation for July 31, 2017 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition For Orders A) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Named Successor; And B) Compelling Trustee Vitor Moye To : 1) Provide Trust Accounting; 2) Re
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Continued To Dec-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Parnall for Kenneth Moye. David Tate for Victor Moye.

Mr. Tate reported that the accounting was provided last week. Mr. Parnall stated that it was schedules only, that there is nothing before the Court and that the accounting was to go back to the date of death but did not. The Court stated that it is clear that they are entitled to an accounting from the date of the last death and that the petition requested that the accounting be submitted to the court. Mr. Parnall stated that the accounting should be before the Court and that mediation is premature. The Court stated that it will continue the matter for status on filing a petition to approve a proper probate accounting and that everything else will be pending until then. Mr. Tate stated that his client will be willing to mediate and Mr. Parnall stated that he thinks his client will be amenable to mediation. The Petition for orders suspending trustee and appointing named successor, etc., was continued to December 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Court ordered that the accounting is to be done prior to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
